symptoms.symptom_name: Annular plaques with a raised scaly advancing border and central clearing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The advancing scaly active edge with central clearing is the hallmark of dermatophyte infection and is directly describable/observable over telehealth video.
Nummular eczema is uniformly inflamed and scaly throughout, without the clearing centre and discrete advancing rim.

symptoms.symptom_name: Centrifugal outward enlargement of the lesions over days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive outward ring expansion reflects the fungus growing radially through the keratin.
Marked itch is typical of active dermatophytosis and drives the secondary excoriation.
Granuloma annulare is characteristically smooth and asymptomatic, not intensely itchy and scaly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Secondary excoriation with pinpoint bleeding at the lesion edges

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical trauma from scratching — a complication of the pruritus, not itself a marker of any specific diagnosis, and specifically not evidence of bacterial spread.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy outdoor soil/grass exposure with dirt under the nails

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Soil and animal/grass contact is a recognised source of geophilic and zoophilic dermatophytes in children.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever and systemic illness; edges not hot, indurated or honey-crusted

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The negative red-flag screen (afebrile, systemically well, no confluent induration or honey crust/pus) makes an active secondary bacterial infection unlikely and supports keeping triage low.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warmth / induration / fluctuance on palpation of the lesion edges

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Definitive exclusion of cellulitis/abscess needs a clinician's palpation, which telehealth cannot provide — hence the safety-net rather than a categorical rule-out.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin scraping for KOH microscopy / fungal culture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Demonstrating dermatophyte hyphae confirms the diagnosis — a laboratory investigation never available in real time over telehealth and not required before empirical treatment of a classic lesion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin scraping for KOH microscopy and fungal culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the dermatophyte and guides therapy if the diagnosis is uncertain, the disease is extensive/refractory, or an oral antifungal is being considered — but is NOT required before starting empirical topical treatment of a classic annular lesion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial wound swab / commencement of oral antibiotics
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated in a systemically-well, afebrile child whose bleeding is mechanical from scratching and whose lesions lack cellulitis/impetigo features; reserve for if signs of secondary bacterial infection actually develop (see safety-netting).
